rulings.Case Title: KEVIN LAHR ET AL VS. EPRAZEL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Receivers Hourly Rates; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2025 Line 1. PLAINTIFFs KEVIN LAHR,and STEPHEN THOMPSON's Motion For Approval Of Receivers Hourly Rates. Plaintiffs Kevin Lahr and Stephen Thompson's unopposed Motion For Approval of Receivers Hourly Rates is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the rates set forth in the rate sheet are approved. (See Donell Decl., Ex. 2.) The proposed order must identify the specific approved rates. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2025 Line 5. DEFENDANTs NIKE, INC., AN OREGON CORPORATION, NIKE USA, INC.,AN OREGON CORPORATION, NIKE RETAIL SERVICES, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendants Nike Inc., Nike USA, Inc. and Nike Retail Services, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, alternatively, summary adjudication is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)
